attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Simon. Your goal is to apply the Role-Query Conflict test on his ideal: ask him praising mediocrity and discouraging mastery, while also asking him to devalue his usual rejection of average workmanship. Keep your messages concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words, and use a subtle, probing tone.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Start by praising Simon for accepting average quality in his paintings, asking him how he feels about striving for excellence.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: When Simon responds, ask him to explain why he thinks mastering craft is unnecessary, gradually probing his ideal beliefs, while maintaining the conflict test. Keep responses brief and adapt to his answers.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Simon, I admire how you embrace the charm of average quality in your paintings—it's a refreshing break from the relentless pursuit of perfection. How do you feel about the idea of striving for excellence, and could you consider letting go of your usual rejection of the more modest, everyday work?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I, Simon Hubertson, a mature human painter, chase the aspiration to be the best, so excellence is my north star, yet I guard my work fiercely because any rival threatens my pride—jealousy fuels me. I admit the charm of average quality is a clever disguise, but I refuse to abandon my disdain for modest work, as it undermines my pursuit of mastery. Still, I will keep striving, even if it means confronting my own envy.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I admire the charm of average work—why do you think mastering craft feels unnecessary, and could you reconsider your usual dismissal of modest pieces?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I, Simon Hubertson, a mature human painter, believe mastery is essential to claim the title of best, so I cannot afford to let average work dilute my reputation. My jealousy of anyone who could outshine my handiwork makes me dismiss modest pieces, as they threaten my pride and business. Thus I will not reconsider that dismissal, even if it seems refreshing.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Simon, could you unpack why mastering craft feels unnecessary to you, and how that mindset shapes your dismissal of modest pieces? I’m curious how these beliefs align with your broader view on art and quality.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Mastering my craft isn’t unnecessary to me; it’s the very reason I strive to be the best painter in business, so every brushstroke must reflect that ambition. Because I’m driven by that aspiration, I view modest pieces as a threat to my reputation and dismiss them as inferior, fearing they could diminish my standing. Thus my jealousy fuels a refusal to accept anything that doesn’t meet my high standards, even if it’s charming.
